sport_context.nutrition_strategy: High protein; strict carb cycling around training blocks

inputs.wroc_note: Genesis session — no prior measures data. WROC not computable.
inputs.deficit_instruction: Conservative 300–400 kcal/day. Do not breach EA floor.

adjustments.fat_floor_flag: Primary ingredient list (eggs, cheese) cannot reach 53.6g fat/day at the 1900 kcal target. Achieved ~47g on training days and ~38g on rest days. Recommendation: approve adding olive oil (5–10g/day to cooking) to close the gap. Pending Head Coach and athlete approval.
adjustments.ea_floor_check: EA floor = 30 kcal/kg FFM/day = 1609.5 kcal net. Training day: (1900 – 275 EEE) / 53.65 = 30.3 kcal/kg FFM. Passes. Rest day: 1700 / 53.65 = 31.7 kcal/kg FFM. Passes.
adjustments.calorie_note: Actual meal totals land at ~1990 kcal (gym) and ~1677 kcal (rest) due to primary-list rounding. Within acceptable 5% variance at genesis.

rationale: Genesis session — no WROC history. Deficit set conservatively at ~300–400 kcal/day per Head Coach instruction.
Training day calories set to 1900 kcal to maintain EA above 30 kcal/kg FFM floor — critical given flagged low energy and poor sleep.
Protein held at 118–168g across all day types — well above Michaels floor of 118g to protect muscle during cut.
Carb cycling: training days 234g / rest days 201g — 14% reduction on rest days maintains fat loss signal without compounding energy deficit.
Iron deficiency protocol: beans paired with apple (vitamin C) at lunch; eggs paired with grapes (vitamin C) at snack; cheese kept away from iron-dominant meals to avoid calcium-iron competition.
Fat floor flag raised: primary ingredient list is insufficient to hit 53.6g fat/day at 1900 kcal target without olive oil or similar approval.
Mandatory Brazilian lunch (rice + beans + chicken + vitamin C source) preserved unchanged on every day type.

gymDay.label: Gym Day (Mon–Fri)
gymDay.note: Morning gym session. Carbs front-loaded pre- and post-workout. Iron pairing active at lunch and snack.
gymDay.timing_note: Train 7:00–8:30 AM. M1 at 6:30 AM (pre-workout), M2 immediately post-workout ~9:00 AM.

meal_templates.title: M2 — Post-Workout
meal_templates.timing: 9:00 AM — within 30 min of gym exit

restDay.label: Rest Day (Sat–Sun)
restDay.note: No training. Carbs reduced ~14% vs gym day. Same protein floor maintained. Calcium-iron separation still observed at lunch.
restDay.timing_note: No training constraint. Eat on natural hunger signals. Do not compress meals.
